pr.title: Auto-regenerate the wiki Translation Units page in CI
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Added a GitHub Actions `update-wiki` job to `.github/workflows/build.yml` that runs after `build-ninja` on pushes to the default branch of `doldecomp/melee`, downloads the existing `GALE01_report` artifact, regenerates the Translation Units wiki page with `tools/wiki_tu.py`, and pushes to the repository wiki only when the page changes.

key_files.role: Adds the `update-wiki` GitHub Actions job for automatic Translation Units wiki regeneration.

what_changed.text: Added an `update-wiki` job that depends on `build-ninja` and is gated to push events on the default branch of `doldecomp/melee`, excluding PRs and forks by condition.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:update-wiki needs build-ninja and if condition

what_changed.text: Configured the job with `contents: write` and `actions: read`, using `${{ github.token }}` as `GH_TOKEN` to clone and push to `${{ github.repository }}.wiki.git`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:permissions and Regenerate and push step

what_changed.text: Sparse-checkout was expanded to include both `tools` and `reqs`, and generator dependencies are installed with `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:sparse-checkout tools reqs and install step

what_changed.text: The workflow downloads the `GALE01_report` artifact to `report` and runs `python tools/wiki_tu.py write report/report.json "wiki/${page}"` to regenerate `Translation-Units.md`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:Download report and python tools/wiki_tu.py write

what_changed.text: The push step exits without committing when `git diff --quiet -- Translation-Units.md` reports no changes; otherwise it commits as `github-actions[bot]` and pushes to wiki `master`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:git diff quiet, bot config, commit, push

smart_moves.text: Reused the existing `GALE01_report` artifact instead of adding another build for wiki generation.
Restricted the wiki update job to default-branch pushes in the canonical repository, reducing risk from PRs or forks.
Used a dedicated `concurrency` group with `cancel-in-progress: false` so multiple default-branch pushes do not race wiki pushes.
Avoided requiring a PAT, machine user, or GitHub App by using the built-in GitHub token with job-level `contents: write` permission.
Kept the commit conditional on actual wiki page changes, avoiding unnecessary wiki commits.

decomp_lessons.text: For decomp project status pages generated from build artifacts, CI automation can prevent manually maintained wiki pages from drifting, provided the job is scoped to canonical default-branch pushes and reuses existing artifacts.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:tools/wiki_tu.py generates the Translation Units wiki page but it drifted until run by hand

naming_conventions.text: The workflow job was named `update-wiki` with display name `Update wiki TU list`; generated page variable was named `page='Translation-Units.md'`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Requested installing generator dependencies via `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt` rather than listing packages inline; optionally suggested separating dependencies to reduce CI dependencies.
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: Final diff uses `pip install -r reqs/misc.txt` and sparse-checkout includes `reqs`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Asked whether the token setup had been tested on a fork and noted uncertainty about non-user-associated tokens.
review_feedback.resolution_evidence: Author replied that no separate token was needed and linked a fork wiki and GitHub Actions run validating `${{ github.token }}` with `contents: write`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Later approved the PR and commented, 'Nice work on these, thanks.'

follow_up_queries: Find CI failures or fixes involving `GALE01_report` artifact download
Find later review comments about GitHub wiki pushes using `GITHUB_TOKEN`
Find changes to `reqs/misc.txt` affecting wiki generator dependencies

notes: The diff and PR body directly support the workflow behavior and changed file.
The author claims fork validation and links a check run; intake records that claim and link evidence but does not independently verify the external run.
No decompilation source, assembly matching, symbol naming, or objdiff evidence is present because this PR is CI/documentation tooling, not a code-match PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Automate generated decomp status wiki pages from CI artifacts
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a decomp status page is generated from existing build reports, wire the generator into CI after the producing build job, gate it to canonical default-branch pushes, and commit only when the generated page changes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Built-in GitHub token can be sufficient for same-repository wiki updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: For this PR's GitHub Actions wiki update, the built-in `${{ github.token }}` with job-level `contents: write` was used to clone and push to the repository's own `.wiki.git`, avoiding a PAT or GitHub App.

source_update_candidates.title: `.github/workflows/build.yml` owns automated Translation Units wiki regeneration
source_update_candidates.text: As of PR #2585, `.github/workflows/build.yml` contains an `update-wiki` GitHub Actions job that regenerates `Translation-Units.md` from `tools/wiki_tu.py` and the `GALE01_report` artifact after successful default-branch builds.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path ownership fact for future CI/wiki automation changes; route to curator because current source may have evolved after this historical PR.

rejection_notes: Do not treat this PR as evidence for any C decompilation style, symbol naming convention, assembly matching tactic, or objdiff validation practice.
Do not generalize the token behavior beyond same-repository wiki pushes without additional current GitHub Actions evidence.
Do not mark the external fork validation as independently verified by this intake; only the PR author's claim and links are present.
